pairControls: same task prompt inside each pair
same starting state inside each pair

acceptedMeasurement.scope: bounded local-current OpenCode app-generation fixture set
acceptedMeasurement.claimBoundary: This is not a universal token-saving, provider-token-saving, product-efficacy, navigation-benefit, or generated-app-quality claim.

legacySelfRatedCounts.basis: self-rated qualitative review only; retained for audit, not measured evidence

evalProgram.aggregationBasis: original-only; replay results excluded from aggregate signal
evalProgram.historicalCompileTestCaveat: Historical compile/test aggregates are toolchain-confounded and are not apples-to-apples PH win rates.
evalProgram.completionSemantics: Provider/tool completion was separated from generated app external outcome before the final frozen step.
evalProgram.diagnosisInterpretation: Workflow/stack positive signal exists. R1 CLI-only reproduction found and fixed a workflow continuation UX gap: finish surfaced raw final verification/bearshell blockers, but workflow continue did not carry that blocker into its next-action prompt. Remaining historical failures still cannot be fully classified from pruned raw evidence.
evalProgram.closureRecommendation: No further eval or analysis loop is recommended without a new acceptance question and explicit HQ approval.
evalProgram.stoppingReason: Deconfounded runtime smoke does not show PH clearly beating the plain baseline; keep injection effect unproven.

decisionRationale: Accepted 10-pair local-current OpenCode PH OFF/ON evidence found PH runtime injection worse for provider-token total, read chars, tool calls, and elapsed time in all pairs. Runtime guidance is downgraded to explicit opt-in preview while gate/evidence CLI surfaces remain the product center.
